product_overview: Publiora is an AI-powered publishing platform that helps creators brainstorm, generate, publish, and distribute marketing ebooks. It combines a guided AI workspace, hosted reader, claim-link access, and a creator dashboard into one end-to-end publishing workflow.
core_goals: Create marketing ebooks with guided AI assistance
Generate and refine ebook structure and section content in a chunked workflow
Publish ebooks to a hosted reader with shareable URLs
Distribute ebook access through claim links and reader library entitlements
Track and manage ebook projects, publishing status, and reading progress
Manage credits and subscription balance for generation actions

features.name: Marketing landing
features.description: Public site that introduces the product and encourages visitors to sign in, register, or start creating an ebook.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Scroll through the product features and pricing -> Decide to sign in or register
Navigate to / -> Review how the product works -> Choose to start creating an ebook after learning the value proposition

features.name: Authentication
features.description: Email and password based account access for creators, including registration, login, password reset, and sign out.
user_flows: Navigate to /register -> Enter name, email, and password -> Submit the registration form -> See the account created flow complete
Navigate to /login -> Enter email and password -> Submit the login form -> See the dashboard
Navigate to /forgot-password -> Enter email -> Request a password reset link -> Receive confirmation that the reset request was sent
Access the app while signed in -> Open the top bar account menu -> Sign out -> See the login page

features.name: Dashboard overview
features.description: Creator home screen that summarizes credits, projects, and published ebooks, and provides quick access to billing or project creation.
user_flows: Navigate to /dashboard -> View credit balance and project summary -> Open a recent project
Navigate to /dashboard -> Review published ebook stats -> Go to billing settings
Navigate to /dashboard -> Start a new ebook project from the overview

features.name: Project list and create
features.description: Project management area where creators can search, filter, and create new ebook projects from templates or a blank start.
user_flows: Navigate to /projects -> Search or filter the project list -> Open a matching project
Navigate to /projects/new -> Choose an ebook type -> Pick a template or start blank -> Fill in the brief -> Create the project
Navigate to /projects -> Review project statuses -> Open a draft to continue working

features.name: AI workspace generation loop
features.description: Core ebook creation workspace where creators chat with the strategist, generate and approve outlines, generate sections, refine content, generate titles and CTAs, publish, or delete a project.
user_flows: Navigate to /projects/[id] -> Send a message to the strategist -> Receive a strategic reply -> Continue the conversation until the ebook idea becomes clear
Navigate to /projects/[id] -> Generate an outline -> Review the proposed title, subtitle, chapters, and sections -> Edit the outline if needed -> Approve the outline
Navigate to /projects/[id] -> Generate ebook sections one by one -> Review generated content in the editor -> Enhance or edit a section -> Save the updated section
Navigate to /projects/[id] -> Generate title options -> Generate CTA options -> Choose the best-fit messaging for the ebook
Navigate to /projects/[id] -> Publish the ebook with a slug -> See the project move into the published state
Navigate to /projects/[id] -> Delete the project -> Confirm the removal

features.name: Claim link and library
features.description: Distribution flow that lets readers claim access to a published ebook, adds it to their library, and lets them continue reading later.
user_flows: Navigate to /claim/[token] -> Preview the ebook claim page -> Log in or register if needed -> Claim the ebook -> See the ebook added to the library -> Open the reader
Navigate to /published/[id] -> Review existing claim links -> Create a new claim link -> Copy the shareable link
Navigate to /library -> View claimed ebooks -> Open a book from the personal library -> Continue reading from saved progress
Navigate to /read/[slug] -> Read the ebook online -> Move through sections using chapter navigation -> See reading progress persist

features.name: Billing and credits
features.description: Billing area where creators can inspect balance, compare plans, purchase credit packs, change plan, and review transaction history.
user_flows: Navigate to /settings/billing -> View current balance and plan details -> Review monthly credit grant information
Navigate to /settings/billing -> Select a different subscription plan -> Confirm the change -> See the updated plan and balance
Navigate to /settings/billing -> Choose a credit pack -> Purchase the pack -> See the balance increase
Navigate to /settings/billing -> Review credit transaction history -> Inspect prior grants, spends, and purchases

routes.description: Marketing landing with hero, features, how it works, pricing
Creator overview with credits, projects, published stats
Project list with search and status filters
Create project with ebook type, template, brief
Workspace tabs chat outline sections preview tools publish
Manage claim links and exports for published ebook
Plans, credit packs, balance, transaction history
Preview and claim ebook via token into library
Hosted reader with section navigation and progress

known_limitations.issue: PDF/EPUB export is stub job status not real file download
known_limitations.impact: Export UI works but files are not production-ready

known_limitations.issue: Stripe not wired; pack purchase uses CREDITS_MOCK_TOPUP
ebook_type migration must be applied on Supabase cloud before create project in live mode
known_limitations.impact: Create project fails if column missing
